response.justification: Bogdan brings a genuinely relevant geospatial background (founder of GISCollective, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic, plus deep Linux and testing discipline. However, his C++ exposure is brief and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2007-2008 and a thesis project), he lists no Rust, his systems-language work is in DLang, and his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node full-stack and web product work rather than routing pipelines or navigation guidance. He is adjacent to the role rather than squarely in it.
strengths: Long-running geospatial domain experience (GISCollective founder, Green Map GIS platform, FOSS4G talk)
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, test tooling authorship, Linux fluency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly 18 years old; no Rust listed
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance systems experience
Last ~10 years are primarily TypeScript/Node web and AI tooling, far from C++ data-pipeline work
